entry.title: SSAM Offline OData: Parent Entity Not Deleted When Child Entity is Local (@sap.isLocal = true)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to clarify the behaviour of Offline OData in SAP Service and Asset Manager (SSAM) regarding deletion when dependent entities are still in the local state.

Scenario

We have the following hierarchy:

  • WorkOrder (Primary Entity)
    → Operation
    → MobileStatus

Current situation:

  • MobileStatus has @sap.isLocal = true
  • This indicates the entity is local on the client (changes sent but not yet confirmed via download)
  • Backend sends a DELETE response for WorkOrder and Operation

Understanding Based on SAP Documentation

From SAP Offline OData documentation:

1. Local & Pending State
  • Local entities exist in the offline store
  • Pending changes are stored in the request queue
  • An entity can be local but not pending (i.e., sent to backend, response not yet received)
2. Sync Behaviour
  • The application operates in the offline store
  • Backend changes are applied via a refresh of the existing store, not overwritten
3. Conflict Handling
  • Server responses are applied after considering the local state
  • Local changes may take precedence until resolved
4. Data Consistency
  • Offline OData maintains referential integrity
  • Prevents deletion of the parent if a dependent child exists locally

 

Observation

Even though the backend sends a delete for:

  • WorkOrder
  • Operation

The entities are still visible on the client

 

My Understanding

Based on the above, my understanding is:

  • Local entities (@sap.isLocal = true) are still part of the offline store state
  • Backend responses are applied considering existing local data
  • If a dependent child entity exists locally,
    → Parent deletion may not be applied to maintain consistency

 

Question

Can you please confirm if the following is correct:

If a dependent child entity (e.g., MobileStatus) is still in local state,
The Offline OData store will not apply backend delete of parent entities
until the local state is fully resolved (synced)?

 

Goal

We want to confirm whether this behaviour is:

  • Standard Offline OData design, OR
  • Something specific to configuration or data state

 

 

Any clarification or references would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: 1H 2026 SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors: Connected Solutions and New AI-First Capabilities
entry.content:
With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered new capabilities within SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors to improve how organizations intelligently attract and hire the best talent.  

To learn more watch the on-demand Q&A webinars: 1H 2026 Product Release Webinars and Recruiting & Onboarding 1H 2026 Customer Release Q&A. To hear more about the roadmap and broader view of our strategy, watch the 1H 2026 VIP Virtual Program for Strategic Roadmap Updates.    

All of the SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 release items can be found on the What’s New Viewer. These new features will be live in Production on May 15th, 2026.

With the 1H 2026 release, we have delivered a deeper integration between SAP SuccessFactors HCM and SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors as well as updates to Winston, SmartRecruiters’ suite of AI products, which are now available for SAP SuccessFactors customers.

Our integration with SmartRecruiters allows for more consistency and synergy across both products and allows our customers to take advantage of the latest recruiting AI in candidate conversion and team engagement.

Now let’s explore the new features, including a deeper integration between SAP SuccessFactors and SmartRecruiters as well as updates to Winston products such as Companion, Chat, and Match.

SmartRecruiters & SAP SuccessFactors Integration

SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors now have a native integration with HCM sync and SSO login. That means a seamless, unified experience across both ecosystems with easy connection to Employee Central and Onboarding modules.

With these solutions connected, you can look forward to greater operational efficiency that eliminates the need to manually enter data or replicate work.

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors Launch

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors is now fully available for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting customers, bringing conversational AI capabilities into your current solution to elevate candidate engagement and experiences.

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors runs on your career site to help recruiting teams engage candidates at scale with generative AI, from job discovery and applications to screening and interview scheduling.

The entire candidate journey is delivered conversationally, so every action is as easy as sending a message to a friend. For example, a candidate looking for a job could simply ask Winston ‘do you have any sales jobs in Seattle?’ and receive an answer directly in the chat.

Look forward to boosting candidate conversion and reducing administrative work with these feature highlights:

  • Chat available 24/7
  • Customizable avatar, colors, and tone of voice
  • Conversational job discovery, applications, and screening questions
  • Automated interview scheduling based on criteria like answers to screening questions.
  • Dashboard to review active bots and performance metrics

Winston Companion: Q&A Agent

Delivered within the core SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors solution, customers benefit from Winston’s enhanced AI capabilities that streamline recruiting processes and drive better business outcomes. Winston Companion’s Q&A agent is your hiring team’s one-stop shop for instant, trustworthy recruiting system and process answers. Users can ask questions without leaving their workflow, rather than opening new tabs and manually looking through article after article.

This convenient new feature helps recruiters save time and stay on task with the following feature highlights:

  • Ask questions about the recruiting process or system directly in Winston Companion.
  • Get instant, contextualized answers with link references to ensure accuracy and allow you to dive deeper.

Winston Companion: Auto Self-Scheduling

Winston Companion’s Auto Self-Scheduling gives hirers a fast, low‑touch way to move qualified candidates from shortlist to confirmed interviews, without the usual back‑and‑forth over email or chat.

By combining preconfigured interview templates, calendar availability, and automation rules, Winston Companion can trigger one‑click scheduling flows directly from a conversational prompt, whether a hirer is working in the web app or via mobile channels like WhatsApp.

Look forward to increased efficiency with the following key features:

  • Template‑driven automation that uses existing interview templates and self-scheduling rules to send out accurate, consistent invites
  • Bulk invitations for high‑volume roles

Winston Match: Subscores

Winston Match subscores give recruiters a holistic, nuanced view of how each candidate’s skills, experience, and education contribute to their overall Match Score. Instead of a single aggregate number, subscores expose the underlying signals Winston uses to evaluate fit, so teams can see not just who is ranked highly, but why they’re ranked that way.

For high-volume screening, subscores make Winston Match insights instantly usable in list views. This accelerates shortlisting while keeping recruiters firmly in control of who moves forward through the following key features.

  • Subscore dimensions of skills, experience, and education/certifications
  • Easy overview of subscores in the applicant list
  • Flexible UI allows users to hide the subscore column if not needed

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors: Search Jobs via Resume with Winston Chat

SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors: Search Jobs via Resume makes job discovery as simple as dropping a CV into the conversation through Winston Chat. Instead of typing keywords or scrolling through pages of listings, candidates upload their resume and let Winston Chat surface relevant roles automatically.

Enable recruiters to reduce friction in the application process while also matching candidates to the most relevant roles, so you can look forward to a boost in high-quality candidate conversion.

Continuous Innovation

At SAP, our goal with each release is to make the recruiting process more efficient and consistent while adding a touch of joy along the way. We want to empower hirers to take time for strategic tasks and meaningful communication that makes your team more visible and valued.

That’s why we are so excited about this round of updates. With the enhanced suite of Winston capabilities, recruiters can leverage generative AI directly within their workflow to boost the metrics that matter to them, like candidate conversion and time to hire, whether that means leveraging Winston Match subscores or SmartRecruiters Winston for SAP SuccessFactors automated scheduling.

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2026 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information

Learn more about SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors.

entry.title: Tyre Tracking Requirement – Design Confirmation & Performance
entry.content:
Our company has a total of 5,000 large fleets and approximately 100,000 tyres consumed per year. The client’s requirement is to track each tyre, including its installation, dismantling, retreading, swapping from one equipment to another, and tyre position.


We are planning to meet this requirement by treating each tyre as a serialized equipment.


Is mapping this process considered a best practice?
Since we have created 100,000 tyres as serialized equipment, will this impact system performance in the public cloud? Should we raise this concern with DAP?

entry.title: SAP Ariba Supplier Management & Risk 2605 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Supplier Management & Risk (SM&R) Community!

If you're interested in learning about some of the new features coming with our May release, you're in the right place. 

Let's take a look at what's new within SM&R this quarter...

 

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance

Additional Authorizations for AI Copilot Joule Scenarios in Supplier Management (SLP-2412)

This feature refines the requirements for user group membership to see prompts for specific Joule supplier management scenarios. This refinement ensures that prompts related to specific aspects of supplier management functionality are only visible  to users with relevant permissions, in customer landscapes that include the applicable solutions.

 

 

 

Ability to Restrict the Number of Commodity, Region, and Department Answers in External Questionnaires (SLP-2382)

This feature enables you to configure the maximum number of answers process initiators can add to Commodity, Region and Department questions in the external modular questionnaire.

Process initiators can only add the maximum number of answers you specify when creating a process, ensuring that processes meet your business requirements. It is applicable for external modular questionnaire. If the data is pre-populated from the registration stage or initial values specified from the template, the selected items need to be within the defined levels in the template configuration. If the pre-populated answers exceed the specified range, you must remove the values that are outside of the defined levels in the template configuration. 

 

 

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk

Additional Joule Capabilities in SAP Ariba Supplier Risk (SRISK-1860)

Your AI copilot Joule integrates with the Risk Exposure API - making it that much faster to find information via natural language prompting.

Take a look at these examples: 

 

Enhanced Virus scanning capability in Supplier Risk (SRISK-1329)

This feature automatically scans all files uploaded during different stages of an engagement request for viruses and malware, providing enhanced security protection throughout the supplier onboarding process. When documents are uploaded during any stage of creating an engagement request, each file is scanned in real-time before the next upload can begin. This sequential upload process ensures thorough security screening of documents.

Previously, you could upload multiple files consecutively without interruption. The system now requires a scan to complete before you can upload the next file. Please allow a brief moment between file uploads.

 

 

 

Enhancing Risk Exposure API by Adding Standard and Custom Category in Response (SRISK-1856)

 This feature enables the Risk Exposure API to retrieve responses for both standard and custom risk categories for suppliers you monitor in SAP Ariba Supplier Risk. The responses depend on the parameter setting for Enable custom risk categories (Application.SR.Risk.EnableCustomRiskCategories) on your site.

The current version (v3) of Risk Exposure API returns responses regardless of the parameter setting. 

When the Enable custom risk categories parameter is enabled, the API returns data for your custom risk categories.

When the parameter is disabled, the API automatically returns data for the default risk categories: Environmental & Social, Financial, Operational, and Regulatory & Legal.

(The previous version (v2) of the Risk Exposure API returned responses only when the Enable custom risk categories parameter was enabled.)

 

Once these features are officially released (May 15th) you can locate additional implementation and technical details, by simply searching for the feature ID here: What's New (via SAP Help Portal)

 

 

​Stay tuned for our additional upcoming blog posts and follow me to stay informed on upcoming feature releases across SAP Ariba.

​​Thank you for being a part of the SAP Ariba community and we look forward to continuing our journey of innovation together!

 

 

​​

 

Resources:

What’s New - where you can find implementation and Technical Details for any of these features (once released on May 15)

SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)

Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)

SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page

SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network

entry.title: Re: Tyre Tracking Requirement – Design Confirmation & Performance
entry.content: Treating each tyre as serialized equipment is a common approach for full lifecycle tracking, especially when you need history like install/dismantle and position changes. From a design perspective, it makes sense. That said, with ~100k objects per year, it’s definitely worth validating performance and data volume impact in the public cloud. I’d recommend discussing it with DAP early, just to be safe.

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: What's New for SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development
entry.content:
In the 1H 2026 release, we continue to focus on how organizations plan, develop, and grow their talent. AI agents and new experiences support how talent foundations help organizations develop the skills and capabilities needed for future growth.

We’re excited to announce the Career Development Agent, now available to make employee growth easier with AI-driven insights. It offers a conversational way for employees to get career questions answered and helps close skill gaps faster by automatically creating personalized development plans tailored to target roles. By making career paths more transparent and aligned with employees’ current skills and traditional role progressions it guides meaningful growth. Identifying viable next roles and clearly outlining the capabilities needed to advance gives employees confidence to move forward while enabling organizations to drive talent mobility.
👀  Check out this demo to see the agent in action: Demo: Career Development Agent 

Also with this release, we are introducing the Succession Planning Agent, which transforms succession by continuously evaluating the health of succession plans and offering clear, actionable recommendations to secure talent pipelines reducing manual work for all succession planners. It helps to proactively mitigate succession risks and make data informed decisions faster, supporting retention and engaging top talent while optimizing where succession resources are allocated. The result is a stronger, more resilient talent pipeline that keeps organizations prepared for change.
🌟  Take a look at the demo to learn more: Demo: Succession Planning Agent 

We are further enhancing the succession experience with deeper EU-aligned compliance, enhanced consent management, and improved filtering. Administrators can now use role-based permissions to control the visibility of overall performance ratings in AI-assisted successor insights, preventing sensitive information from being exposed to unauthorized users. Also, generic object and foundation object filters are now supported in Position Tile live slides allowing users to efficiently filter and present positions in Presentations.

Additionally, enhanced personalized recommendations leverages advanced AI for learning, roles, and career paths, providing consistent, personalized growth at scale. Automatically generated suggestions adapt by career level, location, skills, interests, proficiency, and organizational priorities, helping to ensure recommendations align with company rules and goals. Employees receive skill-focused learning suggestions that drive meaningful development and a more relevant, user-friendly experience to support career progression and build critical capabilities across the organization.

Explore more innovations across the suite in the 1H 2026 release by checking out the release highlights brochure, overview video featuring Bianka Woelke and much more on the Product Release & Road Map Information page.

Learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Career and Talent Development

entry.title: SAP Developer News for April 23rd, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS 

Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/migration-tool-for-your-sap-build-apps-frontend-web-applications/ba-p/14368805  
  • SAP Help page https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/sap-build-apps-path-forward#migrating-from-the-sap-build-apps-standalone-offering-to-sap-build 

SAP Open Source Webinar: Olympic Sailing on Eclipse Technology – From Closed Source to Open Source 

  • Event info https://events.sap.com/olympic-sailing-on-eclipse-technology-from-closed-source-to-open/en_us/home.html  

SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #3 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/customer-amp-partner-roundtable-for-sap-btp-platform-automation-3-april/ba-p/14374109  

DYK #13 Override default auth while using xsuaa (CAP) 

  • Capire: Authentication in Production – Enforced by Default https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/authentication#enforced-by-default  

CHAPTER TITLES 

00:00 Intro 
00:07 Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications 
01:31 SAP Open Source Webinar: Olympic Sailing on Eclipse Technology – From Closed Source to Open Source 
02:24 SAP BTP Platform Automation Roundtable #3 
03:24 DYK #13 Override default auth while using xsuaa (CAP) 

TRANSCRIPT

[Intro]
This is the SAP Developer News for April the 23rd, 2026.

[Shrini]
Hello, SAP community. By now, you may have seen the announcement about SAP Build Apps deprecation as of March 2026. Understandably, the first question on everyone's mind is, what happens to the apps I am already building? The team has been listening closely to the feedback from the community. To make this transition as seamless as possible, SAP has rolled out a migration tool tool for your SAP Build Apps projects. This tool will transform your existing front-end web applications into a fully structured React or SAP UI5 project. Once transformed, you can easily continue enhancing them within your SAP Build environment using Business Application Studio. If you are ready to migrate, you can reach out to SAP by opening a support ticket with the mtar file of your project, specify whether the app should be based on React or SAPUI5 and authorize the transformation. Detailed instructions on creating this ticket are available on this help page. The link can be found in the description. This tool was built based on your feedback. Let us know how this works for you. Finally, a quick reminder, you will continue to have full access to SAP BuildApps for the entire duration of your current contract.

[Mamikee]
Sailing Analytics is now open source, and if you want to learn more about the journey behind it, there's an upcoming webinar you don't want to miss. On May 20th, 2026 at 3pm CEST, join the technical webinar on Eclipse Azimuth Sailing Analytics, a real-time regatta tracking and analysis tool that has been used at more than 1,000 sailing events, including the Olympic Games. In the session, product owner Axel will walk through SAP's journey to open source the solution, including licensing strategy, internal processing, collaboration with the Eclipse Foundation, and community building efforts. Now, if you're interested in open source, real-world analytics solutions, or how projects like this evolve beyond SAP, this is definitely one to check out. You can learn more and register in the link below.

[DJ]
Are you interested in the Terraform and CLI tools for managing your BTP resources? Sharing opinions and experiences on this topic with others? Then the BTP Platform Automation Customer and Partner Roundtable series is for you. And the third instance of the series is just around the corner. It's on Tuesday, the 12th of May at As usual, there will be some info on latest updates, a round of Q&A, some demos and more. There's a blog
post with further details, a link that you can use to register. It's free, by the way. And also in that blog post, there's a link to content from the previous two instances. See you there.

[Shrini]
Did you know you can override the default authentication behavior for CAP service endpoints using a simple feature flag? By default, when you deploy a CAP service to Cloud Foundry with a production -grade authentication strategy, all service endpoints are protected. This happens because CAP implicitly adds the @requires authenticated user annotation to every service. But what if you want to make some endpoints publicly accessible, especially during testing or for specific use cases? You can disable this default behavior by adding the flag RestrictAllServicesToFalse to your package.json. In this batch request service, setting the RestrictAllServices flag to false makes all service endpoints publicly accessible by default, meaning no authentication is required. However, the createBatch action is explicitly protected using the @RequiresAuthenticatedUser annotation, ensuring that only authenticated users can invoke it.

entry.title: Automating Probation Extensions After Sick Leave in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Introduction

In many organizations, the probation period is designed to give both the employer and the employee a fair window to assess performance, cultural fit, and overall suitability for the role. But what happens when that evaluation period is interrupted? Employee absences, especially due to short term sickness, can significantly reduce the time available for meaningful observation, leaving managers with incomplete information at the end of the probation timeline.

To address this, some companies adopt a policy of extending the probation period by the exact number of days an employee was absent during that time. While the intent is straightforward, the execution can quickly become complex. Tracking absence days, recalculating end dates, and ensuring consistency across the organization often leads to manual effort, errors, and compliance risks.

This is where SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) can play a crucial role. By leveraging automation and business rules, organizations can seamlessly adjust probation end dates based on recorded absences, ensuring fairness, accuracy, and operational efficiency. In this blog, we’ll explore how to design and implement an automated approach to extending probation periods when short term sick leave occurs, helping HR teams move from manual tracking to a more intelligent, system-driven process.

Proposed Solution

The proposed solution is based on standard SuccessFactors functionalities, namely Intelligent Services Center (ISC), Integration Center (IC), and Business Rules.

Below is a step-by-step guide to building it.

Step 1: Navigate to Integration Center and click “My Integrations” to start creating your IC job.

Step 2: Now click on “+ Create” and select “More Integration Types”.

Step 3: In this step, you need to define the integration type. For this scenario, select the options as shown in the screenshot below. Once selected, click “Create.”

Step 4: On the first screen of the IC job, you are required to provide a name, a description, and a page size. Below is an example from a demo system. Once these fields are defined, click “Next.”

Step 5: Next, navigate to the “Configure Fields” tab and, from the menu in the top-right corner, select the “Switch to Field Mapping View” option.

On this page, we first define the destination table, for this scenario, we use “Job Information.”

Next, we configure the mapping from the “Employee Time” table to Job Information. As shown in the screenshot below, only two fields are directly mapped: “Event Date,” which is mapped to the absence “End Date,” and “User ID,” mapped to the “User ID” from the source table.

These mappings are created easily by dragging and dropping fields from the source table on the left to the corresponding fields in the destination table on the right.

Step 6: Next, switch to “Detail View” using the option in the top-right menu.

We will now define fields that do not have a direct mapping but instead require a default value. To do this, locate the “Sequence Number” field in the table on the right. Select it, then in the left panel assign a value greater than 2, for example, 9.

Setting a number higher than 2 helps prevent potential errors. If, on the same day, the job attempts to create a new time slice in Job Information while another record already exists, the system will use this higher value to generate an additional sequence number. It’s important to note that the final sequence may not necessarily be 9, as the system intelligently determines the correct sequence based on existing records.

Now we complete the second action in this step by assigning a default value to the “Event Reason” field for this scenario.

Note: It is recommended to create a dedicated event reason for this use case to avoid unintentionally triggering the business rule (which we will configure later) in other situations.

Step 7: We have now completed the creation of our IC job, so click “Save” as shown in the screenshot.

Step 8: In this step, we will assign the IC job we created to the ISC. Navigate to the Intelligent Services Center page, search for the event “Employee Short Term Disability,” and click to open it.

Step 9: Create a new flow if one already exists for this event. Click “Integration” from the right-hand menu, which will add a new line in the center of the page. Set the “Timing” to “When event is published.” Then, use the three-dot menu (highlighted in green) to save your changes.

Note: It is also advisable to create an ISC rule that limits the process to employees whose probation status is neither completed nor failed (or based on any other criteria relevant to your organization).

Step 10: In this step, we will create our first business rule, which will trigger the Intelligent Services Center event. To do this, navigate to the “Configure Object Definition” transaction and select the “Employee Time” object.

Click to create a new rule, following the example below. Once the rule is created, assign it on Save in the object and then save the object.

Note: The absence type used in the rule example is for demonstration purposes only. Please ensure you use the absence type relevant to your organization.

Step 11: Our final step is to create a business rule that calculates the new probation end date considering the number of days the employee was on sick leave.

To do this, navigate to “Manage Business Configuration” > “Job Information.” In edit mode, scroll to the bottom of the page. On the last line, click to create a new business rule as shown below. Once the rule is created, assign it to the onSave event in the portlet and save your changes.

Conclusion

By combining Intelligent Services Center (ISC), Integration Center (IC), and Business Rules, we have demonstrated how SAP SuccessFactors can be configured to automate the extension of probation periods based on employee sick leave. This approach ensures that any absence occurring during the probation period is accurately reflected by extending the end date accordingly, eliminating the need for manual adjustments.

Beyond improving administrative efficiency, this solution also enhances fairness and consistency in HR processes, ensuring that all employees are evaluated over an equivalent active working period. It reduces the risk of human error, strengthens compliance with internal policies, and provides a scalable framework that can be adapted to other absence-driven scenarios.

Finally, this use case illustrates the power of SAP SuccessFactors standard tools when combined thoughtfully. With the right configuration, organizations can transform complex HR requirements into automated, transparent, and maintainable processes that support both HR teams and employees alike.

I hope this solution helps you improve your Employee Central setup. Feel free to reach out or comment if you have questions, or if you’d like to share how you’ve tackled probation extensions due to employees absences in your own environment.

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2605 Release Highlights
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Community! 

We're eager to share the latest feature release highlights across the SAP Ariba Suite. 

Below, you'll find product area blogs outlining (current-gen) features being released in May 2026. 

SAP Ariba Category Management 2605 Release Highlights

SAP Ariba Invoicing 2605 Release Key Innovations

SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2605 Release Key Innovations

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
If your production system has a large amount of ST03 usage data for the 3 month evaluation period, the following issue could be affecting you.  This was found with two of our larger ERP systems.

In the application log after executing the check, we saw many entries with the following note: "Relevance: MISS_ST03N - To determine relevance automatically, there need to be at least 10 sets of usage data in transaction ST03N. "  So we were not getting accurate results.

After debugging the program, I found a check that stops collecting usage data after 1.5 million records which makes no sense.  After working thru a Case with SAP, I was told to comment out the check and SAP would come up with a fix in a future release.  No OSS note yet.  But with May 31 looming...I thought I would post about it now.

entry.title: Re: Workflow delegation CC roles
entry.content:
Delegation in EC is designed only for actionable workflow steps (approvals).

CC roles are not part of any workflow step—they are only notification recipients. Since there is no action (approve/decline) associated with them, they are not considered in delegation logic.

That’s why notifications for CC roles cannot be redirected via delegation. If someone needs to receive notifications, they must be explicitly maintained in the workflow (CC or dynamic role).

Hope this answers your query.

entry.title: Impacts of SAP API Policy v4/2026 on existing customer integrations
entry.content:
With the release of SAP API Policy v4/2026, SAP reinforces that only published APIs (as listed in SAP Business Accelerator Hub or official product documentation) can be consumed, and explicitly disallows the use of internal, private, or non-published APIs — regardless of whether the system is Public Cloud, Private Cloud, or accessed via SAP BTP.

Many customers have long-running integrations and custom architectures (including SAP BTP-based extensions) that technically work today but rely on internal or non-released APIs.

My questions to the community are:

  1. How is SAP positioning the impact of this policy on existing customer architectures that are currently non-compliant?
  2. Is SAP recommending a concrete transition strategy or grace period, or should customers proactively plan migration to published APIs?
  3. In practice, how should customers prioritize remediation for productive integrations that rely on non-published APIs?

I am particularly interested in real-life experiences, guidance from SAP, or best practices adopted by customers and partners since this policy was updated.

Link to the Policy: SAP API Policy

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP Sales Cloud V2 to a Standalone SAP Analytics Cloud - A Practical Alternative
entry.content:
Great question @MatthiasL. At the time of writing, the SCV2 to SAC live connection wasn't generally available. And to my knowledge it's still in "Restricted Availability" per SAP Help and requires a support case via CEC-CRM-ANA. Beyond availability, the live path inherits all HANA live-connection restrictions: e.g. blending with remote models is disabled at the table level, planning is effectively unsupported through this setup, and key analytics features like Smart Insights, Smart Predict, and NLQ aren't available on this model type.

So for customers who want to mix SCV2 with other sources or do any planning without adding Datasphere to the stack, the proxy/import approach is still the pragmatic answer. And for typical CRM data volumes, query performance on the imported model tends to be excellent as a free bonus.

entry.title: Application Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: End-to-End Guide
entry.content:
Application Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud: End-to-End Guide — Catalog, Template, Authorization, Value Help and Scheduling

If you have spent any time developing in S/4HANA Public Cloud, you have probably had a request along the lines of: "can we just have this run automatically every night?" maybe a nightly cleanup, a mass update, or a periodic data push. Your first instinct might be to reach for a classic batch program, but in S/4HANA Public Cloud, that's not how it works anymore.

The answer is Application Jobs. They are the cloud successor to the old SM36/SM37 batch job world, and once you understand the moving parts, they're actually pretty clean. The problem is that most guides I've come across either uses legacy interfaces or skip over the authorization wiring entirely. If you've ever stared at a blank "Application Jobs" Fiori app wondering why your job isn't showing up, you'll know what I mean.

This post walks through the whole thing, start to finish. We'll build a job that mass-sets the Delivery Completed indicator on Purchase Order items, using the released SAP RAP Business Object I_PurchaseOrderTP and EML MODIFY ENTITIES statements. I chose this scenario because it's a genuine business requirement that procurement teams deal with regularly, and it gives us a proper reason to use EML rather than just reading data.

Here's everything we'll cover:

  1. Implement business logic for the application job
  2. CDS views for value helps
  3. Application Job Catalog Entry
  4. Application Job Template
  5. Identity and Access Management (IAM)
    • 5.1 IAM App
    • 5.2 Business Catalog
    • 5.3 Business Role and user assignment
  6. Scheduling and monitoring
  7. Accessing Application Logs

Old interfaces vs IF_APJ_RT_RUN

Older blog posts and some documentation reference two legacy interfaces: IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT (design-time) and IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT (runtime). IF_APJ_RT_RUN replaces both with a single interface and one method: EXECUTE. Parameters are declared as public class attributes.

This guide uses IF_APJ_RT_RUN throughout.

Step 1: Implement Business Logic for the Application Job

To implement the business logic, create an ABAP class that implements IF_APJ_RT_RUN. Any public attributes defined in the class will automatically become the selection parameters for the Application Job.

Right-click your package in ADT → New → ABAP Class.

CLASS zcl_job_po_delivery_complete DEFINITION

  PUBLIC

  FINAL

  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_apj_rt_run.

    INTERFACES if_apj_dt_defaults. "-- optional: provides default values for the

    "-- selection parameters

    DATA company_code  TYPE RANGE OF bukrs.

    DATA purch_org     TYPE RANGE OF ekorg.

    DATA document_date TYPE RANGE OF bedat.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_job_po_delivery_complete IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_apj_dt_defaults~fill_attribute_defaults.

    "-- Set default values for new templates.

    "-- The framework calls this when a new template is created for this class.

    "-- Leave empty if you don't need pre-filled defaults.

    company_code = VALUE #( ( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ' low = '001' ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD if_apj_rt_run~execute.

    "--- The attributes company_code, purch_org, document_date are automatically

    "    filled by the framework before this method is called. Use them directly.

    "--- 1. Find eligible PO items -----------------------------------------------

    SELECT

      item~purchaseorder,

      item~purchaseorderitem

    FROM i_purchaseorderitemtp_2 AS item

    INNER JOIN i_purchaseordertp_2 AS po

      ON po~purchaseorder = item~purchaseorder

    WHERE po~companycode            IN @company_code

      AND po~purchasingorganization IN @purch_org

      AND po~purchaseorderdate      IN @document_date

      AND item~iscompletelydelivered = @ABAP_false

    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_po_items).

    "--- 2. Set up the Application Log -------------------------------------------

    TRY.

      DATA(lo_log) = cl_bali_log=>create_with_header(

        cl_bali_header_setter=>create(

          object    = 'ZAPJ_PO'

          subobject = 'DELIV_COMPL' ) ).

    CATCH cx_bali_runtime.

      "handle exception

    ENDTRY.

    IF lt_po_items IS INITIAL.

      TRY.

        lo_log->add_item( item = cl_bali_message_setter=>create_from_sy( ) ).

        cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log(

          log                        = lo_log

          assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

      CATCH cx_bali_runtime ##NO_HANDLER.

      ENDTRY.

      COMMIT WORK.

      RETURN.

    ENDIF.

    "--- 3. Build EML MODIFY input -----------------------------------------------

    "    The %key for a child entity must include both the root key (PurchaseOrder)

    "    and the item key (PurchaseOrderItem). The %control flag tells the

    "    framework which fields we actually intend to change.

    DATA lt_update_input TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE i_purchaseordertp_2\\purchaseorderitem.

    LOOP AT lt_po_items INTO DATA(ls_item).

      APPEND VALUE #(

        %key-purchaseorder             = ls_item-purchaseorder

        %key-purchaseorderitem         = ls_item-purchaseorderitem

        iscompletelydelivered          = abap_true

        %control-iscompletelydelivered = if_abap_behv=>mk-on

      ) TO lt_update_input.

    ENDLOOP.

    "--- 4. EML MODIFY -----------------------------------------------------------

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_purchaseordertp_2

      ENTITY purchaseorderitem

      UPDATE FIELDS ( iscompletelydelivered )

      WITH lt_update_input

      FAILED   DATA(lt_failed)

      REPORTED DATA(lt_reported).

    "--- 5. Commit — but only if something actually succeeded --------------------

    DATA(lv_failed_count) = lines( lt_failed-purchaseorderitem ).

    DATA(lv_total_count)  = lines( lt_po_items ).

    IF lv_failed_count < lv_total_count.

      COMMIT ENTITIES

        RESPONSE OF i_purchaseordertp_2

        FAILED   DATA(lt_commit_failed)

        REPORTED DATA(lt_commit_reported).

    ENDIF.

    "--- 6. Log summary ----------------------------------------------------------

    TRY.

      lo_log->add_item(

        cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(

          severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_status

          text     = |PO Delivery Completion Job finished: | &

                     |{ lv_total_count - lv_failed_count } item(s) updated, | &

                     |{ lv_failed_count } item(s) failed.| ) ).

      "--- 7. Log individual failures --------------------------------------------

      LOOP AT lt_failed-purchaseorderitem INTO DATA(ls_fail).

        lo_log->add_item(

          cl_bali_free_text_setter=>create(

            severity = if_bali_constants=>c_severity_error

            text     = |Failed: PO { ls_fail-%key-purchaseorder } | &

                       |Item { ls_fail-%key-purchaseorderitem }| ) ).

      ENDLOOP.

      "--- 8. Surface RAP BO validation messages (e.g. "document locked") --------

      LOOP AT lt_reported-purchaseorderitem INTO DATA(ls_rep).

        lo_log->add_item(

          cl_bali_exception_setter=>create( exception = CAST #( ls_rep-%msg ) ) ).

      ENDLOOP.

      "--- 9. Save log — linked to the current application job ------------------

      cl_bali_log_db=>get_instance( )->save_log(

        log                        = lo_log

        assign_to_current_appl_job = abap_true ).

    CATCH cx_bali_runtime.

      "handle exception

    ENDTRY.

    COMMIT WORK.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Step 2: Value Help CDS Views

When a user fills in the job parameters before scheduling, we want proper F4 dialogs rather than free-text fields. The Application Job Catalog Entry supports assigning a CDS view as the value help for each parameter.

We'll wrap two released SAP views,  I_CompanyCode and I_PurchasingOrganization, in thin custom CDS views, Z_I_CompanyCodeVH and Z_I_PURCHORG_VH.

Make sure to include @ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP in both views. Without it, the catalog entry editor will not allow you to select them as value helps.

Company Code value help

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Company Code'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP

@search.searchable: true

define view entity Z_I_CompanyCodeVH as select from I_CompanyCode

{

  @search.defaultSearchElement: true

  key CompanyCode,

  @search.defaultSearchElement: true

  CompanyCodeName

}

Purchasing Organization value help

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Purchasing Organization'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

@ObjectModel.dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP

@search.searchable: true

define view entity Z_I_PURCHORG_VH as select from I_PurchasingOrganization

{

  @search.defaultSearchElement: true

  key PurchasingOrganization,

  @search.defaultSearchElement: true

  PurchasingOrganizationName,

  @search.defaultSearchElement: true

  CompanyCode

}

Step 3: Application Job Catalog Entry

The Catalog Entry tells the framework which class to run, defines how selection parameters are displayed, and assigns value helps. Since your class implements IF_APJ_RT_RUN, when you point the Catalog Entry at your class the parameter table is automatically populated from the class's public attributes. You don't add parameters manually row by row.

Right-click your package → New → Other ABAP Repository Object → search for Application Job Catalog Entry → Next.

There are quite a few options for defining the properties of your parameters; you can mark one as mandatory, render it as a checkbox, radio button, and so on. The Sections and Groups tabs let you organise how parameters appear on the selection screen. None of this was possible with the old IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT / IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT interfaces.

Activate the catalog entry.

Step 4: Application Job Template

The Template sits on top of the Catalog Entry and is what business users actually pick when they schedule a job.

Right-click your package → New → Other ABAP Repository Object → Application Job Template.

When the template is created, the framework instantiates the class and calls IF_APJ_DT_DEFAULTS~FILL_ATTRIBUTE_DEFAULTS (if implemented) to pre-populate default values. You can see in the screenshot that the default Company Code is already filled in.

Activate the template.

Step 5: Identity and Access Management (IAM)

Step 5.1: IAM App

Even if your class, catalog entry, and template are all perfectly correct, a business user won't be able to schedule the job until it's been wired up through the IAM framework. Until the IAM objects exist, the Job Template simply won't appear in the Application Jobs Fiori app.

The good news: when you create a job catalog entry and a job template, the IAM App is generated automatically. No manual steps are needed here.

Step 5.2: Business Catalog

The Business Catalog bundles one or more IAM Apps into a group that can be assigned to a Business Role. You can either create a new Business Catalog or assign the IAM App to an existing one; both options are available directly from the IAM App itself.

For this blog, I'm creating a new Business Catalog.

Once you create the assignment, you'll see an assignment object linking your IAM App to the new Business Catalog.

Don't forget to publish the catalog - click the Publish Locally button. Once done, the status changes to "Published".

Step 5.3: Business Role and User Assignment

You need to add the business catalog to a business role. You can either create a new one or add it to an existing role. For this blog, let's create a new business role.

We now leave ADT and switch to the Fiori Launchpad. Open the Maintain Business Roles app and click New.

In the Assigned Business Catalogs tab, add catalog Z_DEMO_BC_ADMIN.

Save the role.

Assign the role to a user

Open Maintain Business Users, search for your user, and open the detail page. Click Edit, go to Assigned Business Roles, add your business role, and save.

You may need to restart the Fiori launchpad for the role to take effect.

Step 6: Schedule and Monitor

Open the Application Jobs app from the Fiori Launchpad and click Create. In the Job Template field, select your job template. Give the job a descriptive name, something like Set Delivery Completed – Company Code 1010 – Q1 2026.

Click Step 2. In the Scheduling Options section, either run immediately or set up a recurrence pattern. For this demo, check Start Immediately.

Click Step 3 and fill in the parameters. This is where the value help work pays off: clicking the F4 icon on Company Code opens a searchable list instead of a blank text field. The default value we set in the class is pre-filled as well.

Click Schedule.

Checking the results

If the job finishes without errors, the status shows as "Finished", otherwise "Failed". You can also copy a job, cancel a running one, or restart a job chain directly from the app.

Step 7: Accessing Application Logs

  • Application Jobs Fiori app - once the job finishes, click the entry to open the detail view, then click the Log Status to view the application log messages.

  • Display Application Logs app / SLG1 - search across jobs by log object, sub-object, user, date, or severity.

What we built — a quick recap

Object

Type

Name

Job Class

ABAP Class

ZCL_JOB_PO_DELIVERY_COMPLETE

Value Help - Company Code

CDS View Entity

Z_I_CompanyCodeVH

Value Help - Purchasing Org

CDS View Entity

Z_I_PURCHORG_VH

Catalog Entry

Appl. Job Catalog Entry

Z_DEMO_PO_JOB_CATALOG_ENTRY

Template

Appl. Job Template

Z_DEMO_PO_JOB_TEMPLATE

IAM App

IAM App (BG)

Z_DEMO_PO_JOB_CATALOG_ENTRY_SAJC

Business Catalog

Business Catalog

Z_DEMO_BC_ADMIN

Business Role

Business Role

Z_DEMO_ADMIN

Hope this saves you some of the time I spent piecing this together.

Further Reading

The following resources complement this guide, either as the official reference, or as community posts covering adjacent patterns and earlier interface versions.

SAP Official Documentation

  • Application Jobs — SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - the official SAP Help Portal reference covering the framework architecture, interfaces, and configuration options.
  • Application Logs

Related SAP Community Posts

  • How to Implement Application Jobs which Automatically Update Sales Orders - WesleyZhang, July 2024. A comprehensive end-to-end walkthrough covering catalog entry, template, IAM, and Business Role setup. Uses the older IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT / IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT interface pattern predating the 2502 release.
  • How to Do Background Scheduling with Clean Core, Including Fiori Apps - SAP, April 2025. SAP's introduction of IF_APJ_RT_RUN - the single unified interface that replaces the older dual-interface pattern, available from Public Cloud release 2502 onwards.
  • F4 Help for Application Job in Public Cloud (2502 Release) - SAP, February 2025. Covers the value help improvements introduced in the 2502 release, directly relevant to the CDS value help approach used in this guide.
  • How to Schedule Application Jobs from a RAP-Based Business Object - SAP, December 2022. If you want to go further and trigger Application Jobs programmatically from within a RAP Business Object rather than via the Fiori scheduling UI, this post covers the API-based approach.

entry.title: Unified Supplier Management STAT
entry.content:
Imagine you are a heart surgeon about to begin a complex procedure. The operating room is prepped and the patient is ready.

At that exact moment, you have to send an email to a primary care physician for records, call a cardiologist for a diagnosis, and frantically search online for the latest AI-driven surgical planning tools.

Sounds ridiculous, right? But for many procurement professionals, this "emergency room" scramble for supplier information is a daily reality.

For many procurement organizations today, supplier data is scattered across disconnected ERPs, niche apps, spreadsheets, and literal file cabinets. Performance data—if it exists—is often unstructured, making "apples-to-apples" comparisons impossible. Risk monitoring usually requires logging into unfamiliar third-party systems, and collaboration is a disjointed, repetitive mess for both you and your suppliers.

Why does this matter? Because in the age of AI and autonomous procurement, your results are only as good as your data. AI agents can’t improve your throughput or efficiency if they are working with a fragmented "medical record."

To quantify the impact of this fragmented systems and data approach, SAP partnered with Ardent Partners - a next-generation analyst firm that leverages its data-driven research to help business executives make smarter decisions and achieve better results - to research dozens of procurement organizations. The findings were clear. Companies that prioritize a unified approach to supplier management see incremental value across a number of KPIs including:

  • 22% more compliant spend
  • 61% more enabled suppliers
  • 73% higher savings targets

Register to join us 28 May 2026 for the Ardent Partners webinar, Supplier Management: The Integrated Advantage where we'll dive into the research findings to reveal why integrated supplier management consistently outperforms across cost, risk, and value metrics. You will also hear a firsthand perspective from Sonae Arauco on their journey toward unified supplier management and how it aligns with broader enterprise goals.

In the operating room, success depends on having the right information at the right time. Your procurement strategy deserves that same level of precision and readiness.

entry.title: How can I remove the File Name in the Subreport tab?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using SAP Crystal Reports 2016 and have a question regarding subreports.

I created a report by inserting a subreport using "Insert Subreport → Choose an existing report". After inserting it, I noticed that when I right-click the subreport and go to:

Format Subreport → Subreport tab

there is a File Name field that still contains the original file path of the subreport.

The issue is that this file was only a temporary file and has already been deleted. However, the file path information still remains in the main report.

Even though the original file no longer exists, the report runs and displays correctly without any errors.

My questions are:

Is it possible to remove or clear the File Name (file path) from the subreport properties?
Could leaving this invalid file path cause any potential issues in the future, even if the report currently works fine?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices regarding this.

entry.title: The application 'Test your Processes' is not running.
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public cloud Edition ,When running in the app 'Test your Processes', if you run a test process created in the 'Manage Your Test Processes' app, the percentage number should gradually increase, but it stops at 0%.

How can this be resolved?

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.Create own test processes with the "Manage Your Test Processes" app. This time, I registered the procedures for the J58 scope item myself.

2.Run the created test process in the 'test your processes' app

3.It stays at '0% complete, in process' and doesn't move for a whole day.

entry.title: How to Exclude Purchase Account (EIN) from Material Ledger (ML) Closing
entry.content:
We are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Purchase Account (transaction key EIN/EINKK) activated.

While performing the Material Ledger (ML) closing, I noticed that ML settlement postings are being generated against the Purchase Account. I only needed the pure purchase amount in this account, but I found that settlement amounts are being distributed to it as well.

Could anyone who knows how to prevent the ML closing from posting to the Purchase Account please share the solution?

Thank you.

entry.title: 生产订单是否可以提前下到下一个月，对财务是否有影响

entry.title: 切换到 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中全新的业务伙伴主数据批量处理的应用
entry.content:
前言

你是否仍在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中使用已被弃用的业务伙伴（Business Partner，简称 BP）主数据批量导入和批量维护应用？

现在正是时候切换到新的继任应用（Successor Apps），以便在旧应用被正式移除之前完成系统准备。本篇文章将为你介绍：

  • 为什么需要进行切换
  • 新应用带来的主要改进
  • 切换所需的具体步骤和注意事项

⚠️ 重要提醒
在旧应用被彻底下线之前，你必须完成本文描述的切换操作。

当前计划：旧应用将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608 版本中移除。

为什么要切换到新的批处理应用？

新的业务伙伴主数据批处理应用在多个方面得到了显著增强：

1. 更好的易用性（Usability）

  • 启动批量变更流程时，提供了全新的对话框
  • 可以更加灵活地定义和调整选择条件
  • 选择条件可在页面顶部直接查看和进一步细化
  • 不再需要单独定义变更“范围（Scope）”

➡️ 整体操作更加直观、符合业务用户的使用习惯。

 

 

2. 批量变更与批量导入更加集中

  • 现在可以在同一个起始对话框中：
    • 直接基于文件执行批量变更
    • 或进行批量数据导入
  • “管理批量维护（Manage Mass Maintenance）” 应用成为统一入口
  • 不再需要单独的 “Start Mass Processing” 磁贴

3. 更丰富的流程属性

  • 新版本支持更多流程属性，例如：
    • 到期日期（Due Date）
    • 其他流程相关信息

4. 与 SAP MDG 的更深度集成

  • 对于已购买 SAP Master Data Governance（MDG） 许可证的客户：
    • 可在 “Manage Business Partner – Central Governance” 中查看单个业务伙伴
    • 实现业务伙伴的统一视图
  • 可通过后文提到的 Additional Scope Items 启用此能力

5. 最新的 SAP Fiori 用户体验

  • 使用最新的 SAP Fiori 设计语言
  • 支持通用 Fiori 功能，例如：
    • 与其他用户协作
    • 更一致的交互体验

我需要做什么？（切换步骤概览）

完成切换总共需要 三个基础步骤：

步骤一：完成或清理已有批量流程

  • 请先：
    • 完成所有仍在进行中的批量维护流程
    • 或删除不再需要的流程

📌 原因说明
使用旧版 “Manage Mass Maintenance” 创建的流程，在新版本应用中将不可见。

步骤二：激活新功能并调整业务目录

1️⃣ 激活功能开关

激活功能：

Successor MDG apps for business partners
(MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY)

⚠️ 注意：该功能激活后不可回退，请先在测试环境验证相关业务流程。

请打开系统配置里的Activate Features来激活此功能：

 

2️⃣ 移除已弃用的业务目录

从业务角色中移除以下目录（Deprecated）：

  • Mass Load - Business Partner Master
    SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC
  • Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master
    SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC

3️⃣ 分配新的继任业务目录

添加新的业务目录：

  • Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners
    SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC

这将使你的业务角色与最新版模板保持一致：

  • Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data
    SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST

针对2️⃣3️⃣两步的操作，你可以在维护业务角色中，找到你做批量维护的业务角色，手动删除废弃的业务目录，和添加新的业务目录：

 或者

你也可以在应用“在升级后管理业务角色更改”中，打开“不赞成使用的业务目录”页签，选择被废弃的业务目录

 然后对受影响的业务角色直接做更改。这样操作会直接把被废弃的业务目录替换成后继新的业务目录：

 

步骤三：通过导出完成技术设置（关键步骤）

这是一个强制性步骤，用于最终启用所有新应用。

操作步骤：

  1. 启动应用
    Export Master Data – Business Partners
    （Fiori ID：F6551A）

  2. 定义一个最小范围的导出，例如：

    • 表：BUT000
    • 至少选择 一个业务伙伴

  3. 执行导出

    • 不需要下载生成的文件
    • 只要导出成功完成即可

✅ 完成后，即可正式使用新应用：

  • Manage Mass Maintenance – BP（Fiori ID：F7974）
  • 以及新业务目录中包含的所有继任应用

其他扩展范围（Additional Scope Items）

如果你拥有 SAP Master Data Governance 的额外许可证，还可以启用以下 Scope Item：

  • Central Governance for Business Partner (1N7)

这将为业务伙伴主数据治理提供更多高级能力。

总结

通过切换到新的业务伙伴主数据批处理应用，你将获得：

  • 更现代化的 SAP Fiori 体验
  • 更灵活、更直观的批量处理方式
  • 与 SAP MDG 更深度的集成
  • 为未来版本升级（2608 及以后）提前做好准备

👉 建议尽早完成切换，并在生产系统启用前充分测试。

原文出处

SAP Community Blog：
Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Do you have any good solutions?

entry.title: Re: Tyre Tracking Requirement – Design Confirmation & Per...
entry.content:
Your current design (serialized equipment) is valid and commonly used, but performance validation is essential. Yes, you should raise this concern with DAP early, so they can advise on scalability, indexing, and data management strategies in the public cloud.

If the client is open to alternatives, propose the tyre master + lifecycle transaction model as a lighter option, or a hybrid approach depending on compliance needs.

Instead of treating each tyre as serialized equipment, maintain a tyre master record and capture lifecycle events (install, dismantle, retread, swap) as transactional logs. This reduces the overhead of serialized objects while still preserving history. Use serialization only for tyres that require strict compliance tracking (e.g., high-value or safety-critical tyres), while others are tracked at batch level. Implement periodic archiving of old tyre records to keep active datasets lean.

entry.title: Re: Vendor Master Creation Date Not Available in BP After...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA, the vendor creation date is no longer directly visible in transaction BP. Instead, you need to retrieve it from database tables (e.g., BUT000 for Business Partner creation date, LFA1 for vendor master data) or via change documents (CDHDR/CDPOS). The original ECC vendor creation date is not automatically migrated into BP display fields

entry.title: Re: Handling of Round-Off Values in SAP CAR and Integrati...
entry.content: Round-off values should be treated as explicit line items in CAR to ensure invoice-level balancing. During integration, map these to a dedicated rounding difference G/L account in S/4HANA. This approach is SAP’s recommended best practice, ensuring consistency across CAR and S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: WBS element search and filtering in enterprise projects
entry.content: Apart from the Project Control app, WBS elements can be searched using transactions like CJ13 or CN41N, or via reports such as the Project Information System. In S/4HANA, CDS views or custom Fiori apps can provide flexible search and filtering. For easier navigation, it’s best practice to use consistent coding masks and leverage CN41N for cross-project searches.

entry.title: Managing Content Change Notification Issues During SAP Fiori Launchpad Content Exposure to SAP BTP
entry.content:
With the SAP Fiori launchpad content exposure, you can integrate launchpad content from a product into SAP BTP by using an automatic synchronization. During the automatic synchronization, it may happen that the exposure log receives the following error message “Notification for content channel ‘xyz’ not sent. Manual fetch required” (Message Class: /UI2/CDM3_EXP, Message Number: 399).

As you may not immediately recognize that this issue happened, this blog post provides a way of how you can monitor this type of error message using email notification from SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (SAP Cloud ALM).

In short, in the SAP Cloud ALM Integration & Exception Monitoring app, you do the following:

  1. Enable the ABAP application log for the system          
  2. Add a filter for the error message
  3. Add a notification for the error

As an example, we use an SAP S/4HANA system.

Disclaimer: This article is written from the viewpoint of a consumer of SAP Cloud ALM. If you have SAP Cloud ALM-specific questions, such as how to set up your system in SAP Cloud ALM, please reach out to SAP Cloud ALM directly.

Check the following SAP Help Portal pages to see whether you are affected by this issue:

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Enable Content Change Notifications
  • Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs

SAP Integrated Business Planning

  • Enable Content Change Notifications
  • Display Launchpad Content Exposure Logs

SAP S/4HANA on-premise

  • Manage Content Change Notifications
  • Launchpad Content Exposure Troubleshooting - Error Analysis   

Prerequisites

Before we can start with the parts specific to launchpad exposure in the SAP Cloud ALM configuration, you set up your system in SAP Cloud ALM.

In addition, please ensure that you have the Integration Architect role. See SAP Cloud ALM overview page.

Enabling ABAP Application Log for Your System

First, you configure your system to collect data for the ABAP Application Log.

  1. In SAP Cloud ALM, in the Operations space, open the Integration & Exception Monitoring app.
  2. Now, let’s add a system tile to the overview: In the upper right corner, choose the Select a Scope icon and filter for your system. Select it in the table and choose Apply.
  3. On the overview page, choose the system tile and from its menu, choose Edit Configuration.
  4. For your system, toggle the Data Collection to On.
  5. For your system, choose the arrow icon to go to the details page.
  6. Activate the ABAP Application Log entry.

Adding a Filter for the Launchpad Content Exposure Error Message

Now, let’s create a filter so that we can monitor when the error message “Notification for content channel ‘xyz’ not sent. Manual fetch required” is written to the ABAP Application Log.

  1. In the same screen as above, for the ABAP Application Log, enter the monitoring and events view.
  2. To add the filter, in the Filters table, choose Add.
  3. Enter a filter name. Then, enter the following filter parameters:

    Parameter

    Operator

    Value

    Object

    Is

    /UI2/BE

    Subobject

    Is

    /UI2/CDM3

    Message ID

    Is

    /UI2/CDM3_EXP

    Message Number

    Is

    399

    Status

    Is

    E


  4. Save the filter by choosing the Save button at the bottom of the dialog.

Adding an Event to Be Notified About an Error

The last step is to create an event so that you get notified once the filter which we have created in the previous step detects the launchpad content exposure error message.

Please note that this event will not only be initiated for the launchpad content exposure error message. If you had configured other ABAP Application Log filters in addition, the event will be initiated as well. The reason is that on the date of publication of this blog post, according to 3452860 - How to setup filters for the category "ABAP application log" in Exception Monitoring of SAP Cloud ALM, it is not possible to add filters to the event itself.

  1. To create the event, navigate to the Events tab and choose Add.
  2. As Event Name, choose Erroneous ABAP Application Log and enter a display name.
  3. Define the actions: Activate Create Alert. For example, you can also configure the event to send an email once there is an error written into the ABAP Application Log.
     
  4. Save your entries.

Checking the Event

In the Integration & Exception Monitoring app, you can view all events that you have created and that were initiated on the Alerting tab.

  1. Choose the Alerting tab.
  2. Once the launchpad content exposure error happens, you can see it in the table

Conclusion:

This blog post covered how to proactively monitor HTTP 401 content change notification failures during SAP Fiori launchpad content exposure to SAP BTP using SAP Cloud ALM's Integration & Exception Monitoring app. You have learned how to enable ABAP Application Log data collection, create a targeted filter for message /UI2/CDM3_EXP 399, and configure automated email notifications to detect synchronization issues before they impact your users. By implementing this monitoring approach, you can transform reactive troubleshooting into proactive incident management, ensuring continuous content availability between your on-premise systems and SAP BTP.

If you have questions about the configuration steps or would like to share your experiences with launchpad content exposure monitoring, please feel free to leave a comment below.

entry.title: Re: Document Splitting Error in case of BS to BS posting...
entry.content:
Hi Hitesh,

A single Profit Center works because SAP does not trigger document splitting in that case; it simply inherits the Profit Center to all relevant lines. As stated in SAP documentation: “It (system) can only inherit the account assignments if the account assignment combination within the same document is unique.”

When multiple Profit Centers are used, this uniqueness no longer exists. SAP then switches to document splitting and attempts to derive the Profit Center using the configured basis item categories. In your simulation screenshot, both the deriving line and the line carrying the Profit Center are balance sheet items, so SAP is unable to determine a unique basis for derivation.

For manual postings, this can be resolved by either posting the entries separately per Profit Center or manually maintaining the Profit Center on all relevant line items.

Regards,

Anand Raichura

entry.title: Re: KE28: Additional "Process" section is display...
entry.content:
The additional “Process” section in transaction KE28 (with fields like Post Receivers with Amounts From and Currency Type for Amount Check) is an expected standard change introduced in SAP S/4HANA 2025. It is part of SAP’s ongoing enhancements to allocation and balancing functionality, not an unintended issue. Functionally, it provides more control over invoice-level checks and postings, but does not alter the core KE28 processing logic.

The “Process” section is documented in SAP’s upgrade guides and simplification items as a new configuration area for handling allocation checks and currency consistency. Check this SAP Help document Upgrade Guide for SAP S/4HANA 2025 

In earlier releases (e.g., 2023), these options were either implicit or handled via customizing. Now they are exposed directly in the KE28 screen.

entry.title: Re: Deleting records from replicated table
entry.content:
Hi @silverado23,

In a replication flow, the delete or truncate option takes effect only when the replication flow is restarted.

For example, if you create a new replication flow and ingest data into a local table, the initial load will ingest and write records to the local table regardless of the refresh behavior configured (delete or truncate). These refresh options are applied only after the replication flow is restarted.

Given your problem statement, one possible approach would be to build a parallel SQL view that handles the required selective deletes.

entry.title: Re: Story Reports: Data Export in View Mode
entry.content:
Dear Varun,

thank you for your response and for clarifying the intended principle behind Story Reports.

I would like to respectfully challenge the assumption that, in practice, users always see the same data in Reporting that they can see in the application itself. While this may be the general principle, there are notable and intentional exceptions.

Besides Recruiting, Learning is another prominent example where reporting access is deliberately broader than application access. In Learning, we provide users with aggregated reporting insights (e.g. completion rates, participation statistics, averages) while explicitly not granting them access to the underlying learner level data in the application UI. These reports were intentionally designed to balance transparency with data protection and governance requirements.

From a data protection and compliance perspective, the ability to export data in mass significantly changes the risk profile compared to on screen visibility alone.

In particular:

  • Mass download enables data to be stored, forwarded, or combined outside the system, beyond technical access controls and auditability.
  • This directly conflicts with core data protection principles such as data minimization, purpose limitation, and least privilege access.
  • Even if users are authorized to view certain aggregated information, this does not automatically justify the ability to extract row level personal data, especially in a reusable and persistent format.

In many organizations, strict internal policies and works council agreements differentiate clearly between:

  • viewing aggregated insights for operational or management purposes, and
  • extracting detailed personal data, which is usually restricted to a much smaller and explicitly authorized audience.

If the export capability in view mode remains unchanged, we will have no practical alternative but to revoke access to a large number of existing Recruiting and Learning Story Reports. These reports were created precisely on the premise that users could benefit from aggregated information without gaining access to the underlying data or the application objects themselves. Removing them from self service would represent a significant regression for end users and increase manual reporting effort for administrators.

For these reasons, while we acknowledge the value of the feature for selected use cases, we strongly believe that some form of control (permission based or report level configuration) is essential to allow customers to safely adopt it without compromising data protection, compliance, or existing reporting concepts.

Kind regards
Sebastian

entry.title: Re: User status A is not getting updated when notificatio...
entry.content:
If I’m understanding your requirement correctly, you want the notification created through a work order to automatically receive User Status A. In that case, the first thing to consider is whether User Status A should simply be configured as the initial status in OIBS. That would be the cleanest solution if every notification of that type should start with that status. However, if the same notification type can also be created manually or through other channels—where User Status A should only be applied once it put in process then a more controlled approach is needed.

There are two practical ways to handle this. The most direct and reliable option is User Exit IWO10009. This enhancement is triggered when the notification is saved, allowing you to add logic that checks whether the notification was created from a work order (for example, by validating the order reference field). If the condition is met, the system can programmatically assign User Status A using STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN. This approach gives you precise control without relying on broader transaction settings.

The second option is to ensure that you have selected all put in process or notification in process option in OIBS. In addition , you can also configure a work order assign business transaction to update the linked notification with User Status A. This can work well if the status should align with a defined order lifecycle step. However, it requires careful validation because it may also set the status in scenarios where it is not intended, depending on how the order process is used.

In short, if the requirement is conditional and tied specifically to notifications originating from work orders, the user exit approach is typically the safer and more flexible solution. If the business rule is process-driven and consistent across the order lifecycle, OIBS transaction control can be considered.

entry.title: Usage of Catalog Items outside of Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the following KBA Note which was published yesterday:

3745044 -  Using the Cross Catalog Search , the system throws a runtime dump. - SAP for Me

This note contains the following statement.

 

Cause

Catalog item price field supports up to 23 digits with 4 decimal places

PR valuation price field supports only up to 11 digits with 2 decimal places

if a catalog item is intended to be added to a Purchase Requisition via Cross Catalog Search, the item's price must be created within the value range that the PR valuation price field can accommodate — i.e., a maximum of 11 digits total with up to 2 decimal places. Creating catalog items with prices that exceed this range will result in a system dump when those items are added to a PR.

Resolution

Ensure catalog item prices do not exceed the DEC(11,2) limit when those items are expected to flow into Purchase Requisitions.

 

👉  That raises the question for me: 

➡️  In what situations are catalog items created that are not intended to be included in purchase requisitions? In our company, catalog items always flow into purchase requisitions, otherwise we wouldn't create them.

➡️  Are there companies where catalog items are handled differently? Do you procure catalog items directly without first creating Purchase Requisitions?

 

I would appreciate input / examples of other practical approaches 🙂

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Date Filter Resets to System Date Instead of Custom Date Input
entry.content:
I have defined a custom date input control. I am using this date input control in the filter section of a custom widget.

The problem is that the date filter always switches back to the system date instead of using the custom date defined in the date input control. This behavior does not occur with restricted key figures; it only happens in the filter section of the chart.

It appears to be a general issue with custom widgets.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for restricting physical deletion of S...
entry.content:
Hallo @Harukiy 

We had the same issue on an on-premise HANA system and we took away the delete activity on the authorisation objects V_VBKA_VKO and V_VBAK_AAT. 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Taxability Model - US
entry.content: The Tax Area is  not meant to identify the area or location ( zip codes) where  the employee resides or works . A number of  areas or locations or a number of zip codes can have the same Tax Area.  Tax area is rather a combination of applicable tax authorities with their individual jurisdiction.
